questions.question: Data set: 3, 7, 7, 9, 11, 13. What is the median?
questions.explanation: Sorted: 3, 7, 7, 9, 11, 13. Even count — average the two middle values: (7+9)/2 = 8.

questions.question: Data set: 4, 8, 8, 10, 15. What is the mode?
questions.explanation: The mode is the value that appears most often. 8 appears twice; all others appear once.

questions.question: Which measure of central tendency is most resistant to outliers?
questions.explanation: The median is the middle value and is unaffected by extreme values at the tails. The mean is pulled significantly by outliers.

questions.question: A data set has mean = 50. If each value is increased by 10, what is the new mean?
questions.explanation: Adding a constant to all values shifts the mean by that constant. New mean = 50 + 10 = 60. Standard deviation is unchanged.

questions.question: The mean of 5 numbers is 12. A 6th number of 18 is added. What is the new mean?
Data: 60, 70, 80, 90, 100. A new score of 50 is added. What is the new mean?
questions.explanation: Original sum = 400. New sum = 450. New mean = 450/6 = 75.
